name: The White House
subject: Final Day at the U.N. General Assembly
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b0d45fe10f2468f3a15a2a7728de460bb9ab54e01c3a9b0fc6e33eb3eff3a01eabc38910ded1e704d6c462bee9f0b4a23e497a57acd99727

President Donald J. Trump concludes his time at the 72nd United Nations General Assembly today. The President has had a successful first visit to the U.N. where he has led the United States' delegation and encouraged world leaders to band together to face the many serious challenges facing the world.

-------------

Directing Federal Resources to Help Hurricane Victims

The President is continuing to direct federal resources to those that are affected by Hurricane Maria and to support recovery and relief efforts in areas affected by Hurricanes Harvey and Irma. President Trump spoke with FEMA Director Brock Long yesterday and has approved disaster declarations for the
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b0d45fe10f2468f37f21dc740b74158ff996737e012398accc106c9646c978819a035e003cf8c4dfdb88f4a1421c0e87ad77c655a25ca4b8
U.S. Virgin Islands and
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b0d45fe10f2468f3f630f9e9956d10435b44dfb933975d52a130cf432f1559438dca8e9f9923e004544d688a505c04ef21ef2ea81d44cbdc
Puerto Rico.

First Lady Melania Trump Hosts a Luncheon at the U.N.

Yesterday, First Lady Melania Trump spoke at a luncheon for spouses of world leaders, held at the U.S. Mission to the United Nations. Her remarks focused on the vulnerability of children, bullying, and social media. “The future of every nation rests with the promise of their young people,” she stated, urging those present to “ensure they are prepared to accept the torch of leadership for the world of tomorrow.”

Working Luncheon With African Leaders

President Trump met with African leaders at a working luncheon on United States-Africa relations. Presidents and officials of numerous African countries (Cote d’Ivoire, Ghana, Guinea, Namibia, Nigeria, Senegal, South Africa, and Uganda) attended the meeting. Secretary of State Rex Tillerson, Chief of Staff John Kelly, Ambassador Nikki Haley, and National Security Adviser H.R. McMaster were also present. The President commended Africa’s collective strides in business potential, healthcare (including the battles against HIV/AIDS and Ebola), and fast-growing economies saying, "Africa is a continent of tremendous, tremendous potential, the outlook is bright.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b0d45fe10f2468f32e274caba320d333c79bfa4cc254ad7c9b6b4483794156ced87550e7fd1e172af0cb2ab4c9a0dae360429773ff912d56
Read more

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump, Chief of Staff General John Kelly, and White House Homeland Security Adviser Tom Bossert speak with FEMA Director Brock Long regarding Hurricane Maria's impact on Puerto Rico | September 20, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS and VP TODAY

Today, President Trump and Vice President Pence remain in New York for the fourth day of the 72nd Session of the United Nations General Assembly. President Trump
and Vice President Pence will participate in three bilateral meetings with the President of Afghanistan, the President of Ukraine, and the President of the Republic of Korea. This afternoon, the President and Vice President will hold a trilateral meeting with the Republic of Korea and Japan, and will end the session with bilateral meetings with the Prime Minister of Japan and then the President of Turkey.

name: Team Kaine
subject: New poll numbers >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
| **New poll numbers from Suffolk University show a tough race.**
**Don 't let Virginia lose a key voice in the Senate. [Pitch in now >>](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=Bz9MDcMqkzmvFooNeZe7TXF2a9%2Fzz9bg)**

**[DONATE](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=rf6pPLzF8nbJ%2FMsIYgadZXF2a9%2Fzz9bg)**
---

Derek -- A new poll came out earlier this week, and it gave me a jolt -- so I wanted to share it with our key supporters:

**Only 43% of poll respondents said that Tim Kaine should be reelected to the Senate, compared to 46% who said he shouldn 't be. 11% were undecided.**

We know that Tim is a much-needed progressive voice in the Senate who is committed to putting Virginians first -- and we really can't afford to lose his leadership in Congress. **But it's clear we have a tough race on our hands -- [please make a donation to help us reach more voters and get ready to win this race.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=XkiUAVCwO%2F0sQ2OLpABSe3F2a9%2Fzz9bg)**

The good news is that this is just one poll -- we've seen others that show Tim in a stronger position -- and right now, a majority of Virginians approve of Tim's work. But we know from past elections that approval doesn't always result in a win: In August of 2013, Virginia Senator Mark Warner had even higher approval ratings than Tim does -- _and he still nearly lost his 2014 election._ We can't take anything for granted.

**This campaign isn't a sprint, it's a marathon. That means we need to be fighting for Tim every single day, the same way he 's fighting for Virginians in the Senate. [We can't let up -- can you spare a few dollars to make sure Tim has the sustained support he needs to win reelection?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=OplzsuqCcWQl4ZYJ%2Bs5ybXF2a9%2Fzz9bg)**

[**DONATE**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=xzVXuK0Qr4KTf90HCafwAnF2a9%2Fzz9bg)
---

Tim is standing strong in the Senate against the worst elements of Donald Trump and Republicans' dangerous agenda. But if we're not vigilant, the GOP and right-wing special interests could replace him with an extremist like Corey Stewart, a "mini-Trump" who will simply act as a rubber stamp for that agenda.

**[We're building a massive grassroots campaign to make sure Tim remains our senator. Will you pitch in now to join us?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=IFMTyS8XvPn4XiT3wbS6PnF2a9%2Fzz9bg)**

Thank you for your support,

Keren

Keren Charles Dongo, Team Kaine Campaign Manager
| | Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please [unsubscribe](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=65ZWbk0xZu09oFW8hhg36kbql5bts%2Bin).
---

name: New Hampshire Republican Party
subject: Forrester: Shurtleff, Dems should condemn Rep. Sherry Frost's latest comments
body:
**Forrester: Shurtleff, Dems should condemn Rep. Sherry Frost's latest comments **
**For Immediate Release: **September 21, 2017**Contact: **Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310
Concord - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement calling on NH House Democrat Leader Steve Shurtleff to condemn Rep. Sherry Frost's latest comments urging House Democrats to sit down during the Pledge of Allegiance:
"While it is hardly surprising that Rep. Sherry Frost has blurted out another ridiculous comment on social media, I strongly urge Democrat House Leader Steve Shurtleff to reject her call to protest the reciting of the Pledge of Allegiance in the House. Instead of following Antifa and other anti-American movements, all elected officials in New Hampshire should work together in a spirit of patriotism to improve the lives of Granite Staters. Mr. Shurtleff, reject Sherry Frost and her goofy idea."
###
**BACKGROUND:**
**Rep. Horrigan Facebook post with Rep. clean_body: Forrester: Shurtleff, Dems should condemn Rep. Sherry Frost's latest comments For Immediate Release: September 21, 2017Contact: Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310 Concord - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement calling on NH House Democrat Leader Steve Shurtleff to condemn Rep. Sherry Frost's latest comments urging House Democrats to sit down during the Pledge of Allegiance: "While it is hardly surprising that Rep. Sherry Frost has blurted out another ridiculous comment on social media, I strongly urge Democrat House Leader Steve Shurtleff to reject her call to protest the reciting of the Pledge of Allegiance in the House. Instead of following Antifa and other anti-American movements, all elected officials in New Hampshire should work together in a spirit of patriotism to improve the lives of Granite Staters. Mr. Shurtleff, reject Sherry Frost and her goofy idea." BACKGROUND: Rep. Horrigan Facebook post with Rep. Frost Comments | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Republican Party of New Hampshire 10 Water Street Concord New Hampshire 03301 \--UNSUBSCRIBEALT--

|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12032db/2fde7c66/45d2e559/15694d74/556174611/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTcifQ==/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/north-carolina-must-be-a-leader-in-protecting-our-environment?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em17_170920_climate_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_170920_climate_adv_gq_ful__RC")
---
Friend,

When President Trump pulled out of the Paris Agreement, I immediately thought one thing -- now it's on us.

Clean air and a healthy environment are vital for our future, and this is too important to just give up on now.

That's why, yesterday, we announced that North Carolina is joining 14 other states in the U.S. Climate Alliance. But I want to show that everyday North Carolinians are standing behind this effort.

**[If you agree that North Carolina should be a leader in protecting our environment, will you add your name?](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12032db/2fde7c66/45d2e559/15694d77/556174611/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTcifQ==/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/north-carolina-must-be-a-leader-in-protecting-our-environment?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=2+-+If+you+agree+that+North+Carolina+should+&utm_campaign=em17_170920_climate_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_170920_climate_adv_gq_ful__RC")**

The U.S. Climate Alliance is a bipartisan group of states that is committed to the goal of reducing greenhouse gas emissions consistent with the goals of the Paris Agreement.

From our mountains to our beaches, so much of North Carolina's economy relies on protecting our treasured natural resources.

I'm committed to maintaining the quality of their air we breathe for generations to come. But I need to show that you have my back in the fights ahead.

**[Let me know if you're in.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12032db/2fde7c66/45d2e559/15694d76/556174611/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTcifQ==/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/north-carolina-must-be-a-leader-in-protecting-our-environment?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=3+-+Let+me+know+if+youre+in&utm_campaign=em17_170920_climate_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_170920_climate_adv_gq_ful__RC")**

Thanks for all you continue to do,

Roy | | [**Add your name ->**](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/12032db/2fde7c66/45d2e559/15694d09/556174611/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiMjg0NTcifQ==/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/north-carolina-must-be-a-leader-in-protecting-our-environment?&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=28457&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&utm_content=4+-+Add+your+name+rarr&utm_campaign=em17_170920_climate_adv_gq_ful__RC&source=em17_170920_climate_adv_gq_ful__RC")

name: Shaughnessy Naughton
subject: Stop Trump's war on science
body:
Dear Friend,

Donald Trump and his administration are waging a war on science.

We're out of the Paris agreement. They've fired practically every scientist at the EPA, rolled back President Obama's environmental protections, and appointed climate deniers to key science positions. They're even censoring the words "climate change."

When Trump took office, Senator Elizabeth Warren took on climate deniers in the Trump administration with this powerful statement: _" I'm going to say something that is really controversial in some places in Washington: We believe in science."_

**[Add your name to stand with Senator Warren and tell President Trump you believe in science, too -- and are ready to fight for it>>](https://act.myngp.com/el/-1632998882935832576/-5796665983565299712?source=ads&subsource=ads_mcginty_lr)**

Something that 97% of scientists agree on shouldn't be controversial. We know that climate change is real, and it's caused by human activity. But at the EPA, Trump's administrator Scott Pruitt is too busy coordinating a "secretive alliance" with oil and gas lobbyists, raising dark money from the Koch Brothers and their allies, and even allowing polluters to use his office to attack clean air regulations.

_This has the potential to set us back decades._ In the past, Democrats and Republicans alike backed clean air and water standards, combating the damage caused by waste and pollution. Today, climate deniers like Trump and Scott Pruitt's dangerous assault on science could undo all the progress we've made.

We can and must work together to stop them. That's why 314 Action -- a grassroots organization focused on electing and empowering STEM leaders to advance pro-science policies -- is fighting back. **Will you stand with us and tell Trump that you won 't let him deny science and harm the environment any longer?**

**[Stand with 200,000 supporters and 97% of scientists on the 314 Action team and send a message: We believe in science.](https://act.myngp.com/el/-1632998882935832576/-5796665983565299712?source=ads&subsource=ads_mcginty_lr)**

Our voices can make a difference. Together, we will hold the climate deniers accountable and make sure we do our part to protect our environment.

Onward,

Shaughnessy Naughton
Founder and President, 314 Action
---
---
| Paid for by 314action.org
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/-1632998882935832576/-5868723577603227648).

314action.org
410 1st St SE Suite 310
Washington, DC 20003

That moment got me started on my mission to expand opportunities for education across Wisconsin, but we have a whole lot further to go. What are the [issues](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=4426a208819242fc9a5f6791aafa2c57&_e=Py7uBTlKqiyd1o1QXLTE_Xtcy46oOGdG4SlgVCQHinj9DbNiklRgSBd376vdnCEQYeJJ5WwksPfbI0eGRYxU5aqPQbBPv_1uHpFkAP4xWRWUZdThhO41gGX_jxyguhhfixCCvTgFRR0D8m_JoXGPyXYNKg9uEC5Sf4axBNZULx0neVYLwx4wNFhFdCUOXW-2BIdL5hKUasNv8xdz8ELYhYyrjcNgV8Blw5weh8jQ1yMElNPKTofLUw3DncNSe3aTRo1-WlHMPO3W2pWdAhcvBHfhbUFCe7wSYfXMNdN3qSl1tLOknmzzGHoilZ8qQNbwdkW0TnwkcxRgqn4TA8lXOjxVnHuANSYbn-chs6gwGKVPlya0sfbIC5LQwkPAX0Im) that you care about? clean_body: I know there's a lot of noise crowding the political stage, and lately it seems too easy for politicians to forget their values. They forget the..... Friend, I know there's a lot of noise crowding the political stage, and lately it seems too easy for politicians to forget their values. They forget the reason they ran, they forget the people they're supposed to be serving, and they forget the reason they were elected in the first place! I can promise you, friend , that I will never do that. I want to work with you, side by side, to change Washington. That’s why I’ve had my team put together this CERTIFIED Wisconsin Values Survey, and I’m asking for your feedback today: I know that the people of Wisconsin are demanding something new from their Senator, and, friend, I’m going to deliver exactly that. The question is, are you ready to help me take on the status quo?? If you are ready, I need you to take this survey now. Friend, I know that if we work together, we CAN bring conservative change to Washington, the same way we did in Wisconsin. Leah Paid for by Leah for Senate

This is really scary. In six days time, we could have a healthcare disaster on our hands. Will you help us defend healthcare with anything you can?
---
**[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/obamacare-repeal-deadline?refcode=DPE-20170921-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**
Team,

This is really scary.

It was just two months ago we were celebrating a health care victory, but in six days time we may have a total disaster.

McConnell's already announced a vote for the Graham-Cassidy-Heller bill. If passed, this bill would end Medicaid as we know it, allow states to price families out of health care because of pre-existing conditions, reinstate lifetime caps, and possibly leave as many as 30 million people without health care.

[**So we 're asking, team. Will you chip in $1 right now to fund our new ads and all of our efforts to support Senate Democrats in this fight? There's too much on the line and we may only have six days.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/obamacare-repeal-deadline?refcode=DPE-20170921-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

We have come so far since the Affordable Care Act was passed. Children can't be denied health care because of pre-existing conditions, health care companies can't charge you more simply because of your gender, cancer patients can't be cut off from treatment because they've hit lifetime caps, and so, so many lives have been saved.

We can't go back and we can't sit on the sidelines because of prior victories. Mitch McConnell only has a few days left to pass health care with a simple majority of 50 and he is actively whipping votes. We need to ramp up the pressure and make it as painful as possible for the GOP to rip health care away from millions of Americans.

**Let 's defend health care with all we've got. Chip in $1 now to ramp up our new ads and all our efforts to stand with Senate Democrats in this fight. Before it's too late:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/we-need-every-democrat-off-the-sidelines**](http://action.dscc. name: Tom Perez
subject: Here's how you can help with hurricane recovery right now:
body:
Here's how you can help those affected by Hurricane Maria
---
Derek --

In the wake of Hurricanes Harvey and Irma, Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands are bearing the brunt of yet another hurricane.

Our thoughts and prayers go out to those who have been affected by Hurricane Maria.

This storm has done catastrophic damage across Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands. There is much that you can do to help relief efforts on the ground in these areas and help people who may still be in its path in this critical moment.

**If you're able to, I hope you'll lend a hand. To learn more about how to help, visit:**

**[HELP WITH HURRICANE RECOVERY](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405235ff/4067b0a/397be788d/7e6377ce/3681646217/VEsH/ "http://my.democrats.org/Support-Hurricane-Recovery?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+HELP+WITH+HURRICANE+RECOVERY&utm_campaign=em_20170921_tp_f2&source=em_20170921_tp_f2")**
Thank you to everyone who has already stepped up to help our friends and neighbors who've been affected by Hurricanes Harvey, Irma, and Maria -- and a heartfelt thank you to the brave first responders who are working to keep folks safe.

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405235ff/4067b0a/397be788d/7e63723d/3681646217/VEsE/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20170921_tp_f2&source=em_20170921_tp_f2"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405235ff/4067b0a/397be788d/7e63723a/3681646217/VEsF/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170921_tp_f2&source=em_20170921_tp_f2"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405235ff/4067b0a/397be788d/7e63723b/3681646217/VEsC/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170921_tp_f2&source=em_20170921_tp_f2") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405235ff/4067b0a/397be788d/7e637238/3681646217/VEsD/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170921_tp_f2&source=em_20170921_tp_f2"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

body:

name: Robert Becker via Our Revolution
subject: Aid for Puerto Rico
body:
Having lived in Vieques, Puerto Rico for over a decade I've seen the challenges
#
Friends,
Having lived in Vieques, Puerto Rico for over a decade I've seen the challenges the island faces, but few can compare to rebuilding that will need to occur after Hurricane Maria. Due to austerity measures forced on them by the vulture funds holding their debts, families were left without the necessary infrastructure to weather these recent storms. Irma has already cost $1 billion, and now Puerto Rico is facing the worst with Maria.
Maria was the strongest hurricane to hit Puerto Rico in almost a century, and has severed communications and crushed its aging electricity infrastructure. San Juan Mayor Carmen Yulin Cruz has indicated it may take up to six months just to restore power to the island's 3.5 million residents.
These hurricanes are not accidents. They are a direct result of climate change. As is the case throughout the world, low income communities and people of color face the challenges not only of storms such as Irma and Maria, but of pollution, water contamination, and a degraded natural environment that exposes them to disease at a far higher rate. This record breaking Atlantic hurricane season, as well as record breaking climate trends across the world, must be our impetus to take aggressive action to preserve our planet now before it is too late.
Please consider contributing to the organizations below to help Puerto Rico rebuild in the wake of Hurricanes Irma and Maria:
[**Help Puerto Rico recover from Hurricane Maria**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af7e/bed43eb/24ce4df/75ffc848/1842719565/VEsH/ "https://www.youcaring.com/variouspuertoricannonprofits-956765"). Contributions made to this crowd-funding effort will go to several Puerto Rico-based organizations.
[**Friends of Puerto Rico**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af7e/bed43eb/24ce4df/75ffc84b/1842719565/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/donate/1661202080577649/"). A DC-based nonprofit run by people of the Puerto Rican diaspora that funds arts, education and culture. They have launched a campaign to send money to Puerto Rico.
[**ConPRmetidos**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af7e/bed43eb/24ce4df/75ffc84a/1842719565/VEsF/ "http://www.conprmetidos.org/"). An organization that aims to connect Puerto Ricans from across the world to build Puerto Rico as a global economy.
[**Taller Salud**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af7e/bed43eb/24ce4df/75ffc875/1842719565/VEsC/ "https://www.facebook.com/taller.salud/"). A feminist grassroots organization that supports health in frontline communities, specifically focused on the communities in Loiza. This town has a high population of low income people of color who suffer the most during natural disasters.
Vulture funds have exploited Puerto Rico's economic woes and forced millions of people to suffer: 45% of Puerto Ricans live in poverty, including 56% of the children. Thousands of people are fleeing the island for the mainland United States every week, where threadbare social safety nets are still better than what can be provided locally under the cruel policies of austerity. Puerto Ricans are Americans, but are not always treated as such. It is a cruel reminder of our colonial history.
The local Our Revolution group is dedicated to rectifying these injustices and more, but they need your help.
I have no doubt that Puerto Rico will weather this storm and come back stronger than ever, to fight with us for a future of racial, economic, social, and environmental justice.
In solidarity,
Robert Becker
Former Iowa Director, Bernie 2016
Our Revolution member

Help us continue transforming American politics and fighting for progressive candidates and causes by [contributing to Our Revolution here](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af7e/bed43eb/24ce4df/75ffc871/1842719565/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-footer-OR").

name: Jay Inslee
subject: We’re leading -- as we always do
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
I'm at the United Nations because climate change needs global (and local!) action.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTE5Nzc2ODUS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI0MTgzNDI5NgS2&mt=1&rt=0) Dear Friend, Earlier this week, I took off from Washington state to New York for "Climate Week" with other leaders at the United Nations. From the airport, we could smell the smoke from the recent forest fires burning across the Pacific Northwest. It was yet another reminder that our climate is in peril. And a pattern of extreme, disastrous weather is becoming our new norm. We're taking global -- and local -- action to combat climate change. And the meetings in New York are a critical opportunity to bring public and private leaders together from across the globe to affirm our commitment to this critical work. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTE5Nzc2ODUS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI0MTgzNDI5NgS2&mt=1&rt=0) [**I shot a quick update for you about the progress we 're making at the UN -- I hope you'll watch. And then I hope you'll take the next step and invest in our movement to take climate action. **](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTE5Nzc2ODUS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI0MTgzNDI5NgS2&mt=1&rt=0) From the fires in my home state, to the historic storms down south, to the terrible flooding in Africa and Asia -- ignoring climate change means the loss of more life and property. It's our moral, and economic, obligation to act. And I'm proud that Washington state is leading with cutting-edge technology and a commitment to the clean energy economy. And I know there's much more work ahead. So thanks for being part of our team. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [WATCH »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTE5Nzc2ODUS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI0MTgzNDI5NgS2&mt=1&rt=0)
